content: -! The relative page has a new look
-- The overall visual treatment has been updated to be consistent with the leaderboard
-- The relative driver's total stint laps is now displayed next to their name
-! New feature: mini orbital tracker
-- You can now choose to display a mini orbital tracker in place of the track map in the Relative page
-- This setting can be found in the Command Spec customizations page of the plugin
-! Significant updates to the telemetry data stream
-- The dashboard is a lot more performant and should run a lot smoother
-- The orbital tracker benefits the most. Driver markers are no longer going to jump around. Class colors will always be reported accurately.
- Fixed an issue with the time table pit marker occasionally jumping around
- Added an out lap indicator in the time table
- The out lap indicator logic has been tweaked and is now more reliable
- Fixed a minor bug in the timing delta widget to account for DeltaToSessionBest null state
- Fixed a minor bug in required/optional repair code that would occasionally throw an error in the Simhub log

- Note: the class positions on the relative panel update at the end of each lap. While this mimics iRacing's relative black box, this isn't an intentional choice and I intend to update it so it shows the live position.
- Known issue: the driver ordering at the end of the race is bugged. This will be fixed soon in the next update.
- Added throttle and brake as an option to the side indicators
- Pit entry optimizer now appears as you're approaching the pit lane instead of relying primarily on pit limiter status
-- This is especially useful in cars like the MX-5 where the pit limiter disengages if you're on the first gear
- Added the pit stop marker on the leaderboard
-- I wasn't able to adjust the visuals so that the text is legible on small screens. Expect this to be tweaked.
- Fixed a minor bug in the timing delta widget to account for DeltaToSessionBest null state
- Fixed a minor bug in required/optional repair code that would occasionally throw an error in the Simhub log
- I've temporarily suppressed the battle indicator from the leaderboard to address a logic issue. I'll have this back online in the next update.

- Known issue: the fuel isn't displayed correctly if you have the Simhub setting set to Gallons. This will be fixed in the next update but if you'd like to use imperial units, set it to Gal in the Mission Control plugin and Liters in Simhub settings. This also effects the refuel helper widget.
- Significant updates to the telemetry data stream
- Added support for the mini orbital tracker in Command Spec
- Added support for throttle/brake input option for side indicators in Drive Spec
- Improved reliability of account linking and validation
- WIP: Laying the foundation for a major update to the underlying data source
-- This will improve accuracy and reliability across a number of features
-- More to follow!
-! New feature: Side indicator
-- You can now customize what you see on the left and ride side of the central dial
-- It currently supports the following: fuel level, timing delta, fuel target +0
-- The default setting displays the static yellow bars
- Resolved an issue with account linking and validation
- Added support for the new side indicator feature in Drive Spec
-- This feature can be accessed in the Drive Spec tab
-! New tab added: Release notes
-- View all historical updates in one place
-- Home page now shows the latest update
- Added links to the Discord server and Patreon
- Misc visual tweaks
-! New feature: 'White flag next lap' indicator
-- The flag box displays an indicator when there's a chance that the white flag has been waved for the overall leader

-- Since iRacing only displays the white flag when you're approaching the Start/Finish line, having this indicator helps you decide if you need to come in for a splash and dash, or choosing when to make a pass if you're battling someone closely and need to know if you have 1 or 2 laps left
-- Note: this is functionally identical to the feature released in Command Spec recently.
- Added a pit limiter indicator
- Added a towing indicator
- Fixed bug where time left would occasionally disappear for a few seconds during qual sessions
- The slow car alert yellow flag now flashes until you are within 100m of the slow/stopped car. This is now consistent with the logic present in the slow car indicator in Drive Spec.
- Tweaked the visuals for the slow car alert so that the linear gauge is easier to see
- Added vehicle settings to the Relative page (see screenshot below). This replaces the pit service menu on that page.
- Fixed an issue which caused the pit service menu to not be clickable
- Fixed an issue which caused the Simhub controls (redraw pit lane/discard reference lap) to not be clickable
- Added a more prominent background for the player's line item on the time table, orbital tracker and driver roster. This helps identify your place on the table(s) a little more easily.
- Minor visual updates to the flag box
- Minor bug fixes
-! New feature: pit exit marker on leaderboard
-- When pit service items are active (refueling and/or tires), a green marker appears on the leaderboard indicating the position you'll likely be in if you pitted that lap (see screenshot below).
-- This marker also indicates approximate time lost by factoring time needed for refueling, changing tires, and driving in the pit lane with the limiter engaged.

-- As with the pit exit marker on the orbital tracker, this feature is available for the following cars/car classes: GT3, GT4, GTP, TCR, Dallara P217, Ligier P320, Global Mazda MX-5 Cup, Toyota GR86, Renault Clio, BMW M2 CS Racing
-- This marker is present on the leaderboard page as well as the orbital tracker page
-! New feature: 'White flag next lap' indicator
-- The flag box displays an indicator when there's a chance that the white flag has been waved for the overall leader (see screenshot below).

-- Since iRacing only displays the white flag when you're approaching the Start/Finish line, having this indicator helps you decide if you need to come in for a splash and dash, or choosing when to make a pass if you're battling someone closely and need to know if you have 1 or 2 laps left.
- The flag box now displays the session time left (when applicable) when no flags are active - this is now consistent with the Flag Box dashboard
- The blue new best lap indicator on the leaderboard and orbital tracker pages is no longer displayed if the driver isn't connected to the session
- The overall leader is now permanently exposed on the orbital tracker similar to the class leader - this makes it easier to find them on the map at a quick glance.
- Minor bug fixes
